content.problemStatement:
You have NNN numbers 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N. Initially, all of them are unmarked, and you want to mark all of them by the end.

There are NNN possible operations, numbered 111, 222, …\ldots…, NNN, where the iii-th operation costs AiA_iAi​ coins and you can use it in **exactly one** of the following ways:


**Prefix Type**: Mark all the numbers in the range [1,i][1, i][1,i]
**Suffix Type**: Mark all the numbers in the range [i,N][i, N][i,N]
Each operation can be used at most once.

Find the minimum cost (in coins) to mark all the numbers 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the upper bound on the numbers to mark.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum cost to mark all numbers.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : You can use the Prefix Type of the 2nd2^{nd}2nd operation for a cost of 111 coin to mark everything in the range [1,2][1, 2][1,2].
Test Case 2 : You can use the Suffix Type of the 1st1^{st}1st operation for a cost of 333 coins to mark everything in the range [1,3][1, 3][1,3].
